2020.subject: English Language
2020.instructions:
This paper contains 50 questions. Answer ALL questions. Choose the best answer from the options A–E. Questions 1–10 are based on the passage below.

PASSAGE: Mohamed is a Class 6 pupil at a primary school in Sierra Leone. Every morning he wakes up at six o'clock, washes his face, brushes his teeth and eats breakfast before walking to school. His school is two kilometres from his home. Mohamed enjoys reading storybooks and always brings one in his schoolbag. His teacher, Mrs Koroma, often praises him because he is hardworking and respectful. Last Friday, Mohamed won first prize in the school spelling competition. His parents were very proud of him.

questions.q: What time does Mohamed wake up every morning?
questions.exp: The passage states "Every morning he wakes up at six o'clock."

questions.q: How far is Mohamed's school from his home?
questions.exp: The passage says "His school is two kilometres from his home."

questions.q: What does Mohamed enjoy doing?
questions.exp: The passage states "Mohamed enjoys reading storybooks."

questions.q: Who is Mohamed's teacher?
questions.exp: The passage says "His teacher, Mrs Koroma."

questions.q: Why does Mrs Koroma praise Mohamed?
questions.exp: The passage states she praises him "because he is hardworking and respectful."

questions.q: What did Mohamed win last Friday?
o: First prize in the spelling competition
questions.exp: The passage says "Mohamed won first prize in the school spelling competition."

questions.q: The word "praises" in the passage means:
questions.exp: "Praises" means to express approval or admiration — closest in meaning to "co...

questions.q: Which of these does Mohamed do FIRST in the morning?
questions.exp: The order given is: washes his face, brushes his teeth, eats breakfast, walks...

questions.q: What does Mohamed always bring in his schoolbag?
questions.exp: The passage says he "always brings one [a storybook] in his schoolbag."

questions.q: How did Mohamed's parents feel about his prize?
questions.exp: The passage says "His parents were very proud of him."

questions.q: Choose the correct word to complete: "She ______ to school every day."
questions.exp: "She" is third-person singular. Simple present requires "walks" (verb + s).

questions.q: Which sentence is written correctly?
questions.exp: "She is a good girl" is grammatically correct. The others have subject-verb a...

questions.q: The plural of "child" is:
questions.exp: "Child" has the irregular plural "children."

questions.q: Choose the correct punctuation: "What is your name ___"
questions.exp: A question should end with a question mark (?).

questions.q: Which word is an adjective in: "The tall boy ran quickly."?
questions.exp: "Tall" is an adjective — it describes the noun "boy."

questions.q: The opposite of "ancient" is:
questions.exp: "Ancient" means very old. Its opposite is "modern."

questions.q: Choose the word that best completes: "The cat sat ______ the mat."
questions.exp: "On" is the correct preposition — the cat sat on top of the mat.

questions.q: Which of these is a noun?
questions.exp: "Happiness" is a noun (a state or quality). The others are a verb, adjective,...

questions.q: The word "slowly" is a(n):
questions.exp: "Slowly" describes how an action is done — it is an adverb.

questions.q: Choose the correct sentence:
questions.exp: "He doesn't like rice" is correct. "Doesn't" (does not) is used with third-...

questions.q: Which word is spelt CORRECTLY?
questions.exp: "Achieve" is correctly spelt. Remember: "i before e except after c."

questions.q: The word "beautiful" has ______ syllables.
questions.exp: "Beau-ti-ful" has three syllables.

questions.q: "She is taller than her sister." The word "taller" is a ______ adjective.
questions.exp: "Taller" compares two people — it is a comparative adjective (formed by addin...

questions.q: Choose the word that means the SAME as "huge":
questions.exp: "Huge" means very large. "Enormous" is a synonym (same meaning).

questions.q: Which sentence uses a comma correctly?
questions.exp: A comma is correctly used after an introductory clause: "After eating, she sl...

questions.q: Choose the correct verb: "The children ______ playing outside."
questions.exp: "Children" is plural, so we use "are" in present continuous.

questions.q: The word "doctor" belongs to which word class?
questions.exp: "Doctor" is a person — it is a noun.

questions.q: Which of these words is a VERB?
questions.exp: "Swim" is an action word — it is a verb.

questions.q: Choose the correct pronoun: "______ is my friend."
questions.exp: The subject pronoun "He" is correct as the subject of the sentence.

questions.q: What is the past tense of "run"?
questions.exp: "Run" has the irregular past tense "ran."

questions.q: Choose the word that BEST fills: "The sun ______ brightly."
questions.exp: Simple present tense with third-person singular ("The sun") requires "shines."

questions.q: Which sentence is in the PAST TENSE?
questions.exp: "Walked" is the past tense form — "She walked to school."

questions.q: The word that means "to make a loud noise" is:
questions.exp: "Shout" means to make a loud noise or cry out loudly.

questions.q: Choose the correct article: "She ate ______ orange."
questions.exp: "An" is used before words starting with a vowel sound. "Orange" starts with a...

questions.q: Which of these is a COMPOUND WORD?
questions.exp: "Football" is a compound word made of two words: "foot" + "ball."

questions.q: What does the prefix "un-" mean in the word "unhappy"?
questions.exp: The prefix "un-" means "not." Unhappy = not happy.

questions.q: Choose the ANTONYM of "noisy":
questions.exp: An antonym is a word with the opposite meaning. The opposite of "noisy" is "q...

questions.q: Which sentence uses "their" correctly?
questions.exp: "Their" shows possession. "They left their bags" correctly uses "their."

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Tomorrow" is the correct spelling — one "m" and double "r."

questions.q: The sentence "Aminata reads every night" is:
questions.exp: A sentence that makes a statement is declarative.

questions.q: Which word completes the analogy? "Hot is to Cold as Day is to ______."
questions.exp: "Hot" and "Cold" are opposites. The opposite of "Day" is "Night."

questions.q: Choose the correct spelling:
questions.exp: "Grammar" is the correct spelling.

questions.q: "We should keep our school clean." The word "clean" in this sentence is a(n):
questions.exp: Here "clean" describes the noun "school" — it is an adjective.

questions.q: Choose the word that BEST replaces "said" in: "Stop that!" he ______.
questions.exp: "Shouted" best conveys the loud command "Stop that!"

questions.q: Which of these sentences is INTERROGATIVE?
questions.exp: An interrogative sentence asks a question: "Is she sleeping?"

questions.q: The word "carefully" is formed by adding the suffix ______ to "careful".
questions.exp: Adding the suffix "-ly" to an adjective forms an adverb: careful → carefully.

questions.q: Which of these is an EXCLAMATORY sentence?
questions.exp: An exclamatory sentence expresses strong feeling and ends with "!".

questions.q: Choose the correct sentence:
questions.exp: "We have a car" is correct. "Have" is used with I/you/we/they.

questions.q: The MAIN IDEA of the passage about Mohamed is:
o: Mohamed is a hardworking and dedicated pupil
questions.exp: The passage focuses on Mohamed's daily routine, love of reading, and achieve...

questions.q: In your composition, which of the following should come LAST?
questions.exp: In a well-structured composition: Title → Introduction → Body → Conclusion last.

2021.subject: English Language
2021.instructions:
This paper contains 50 questions. Answer ALL questions. Choose the best answer from the options A–E. Questions 1–10 are based on the passage below.

PASSAGE: Fatima and her younger brother Karim live with their parents in a small town near Freetown. Their father is a fisherman who goes to sea every morning before sunrise. Their mother sells fish at the local market. Every Saturday, Fatima helps her mother at the market while Karim stays home to sweep the yard. Last Saturday was special because it was their mother's birthday. Fatima and Karim used their savings to buy their mother a new headscarf. Their mother was so moved that she hugged them both and wept with joy.

questions.q: Where do Fatima and Karim live?
o: In a small town near Freetown
questions.exp: The passage states they live "in a small town near Freetown."

questions.q: What does their father do?
questions.exp: The passage says "Their father is a fisherman."

questions.q: When does their father go to sea?
questions.exp: The passage states he goes "every morning before sunrise."

questions.q: What does Fatima do every Saturday?
o: She helps her mother at the market
questions.exp: The passage says "Every Saturday, Fatima helps her mother at the market."

questions.q: What does Karim do on Saturdays?
questions.exp: The passage says "Karim stays home to sweep the yard."

questions.q: Why was last Saturday special?
questions.exp: The passage says "Last Saturday was special because it was their mother's bi...

questions.q: What did Fatima and Karim buy for their mother?
questions.exp: They "used their savings to buy their mother a new headscarf."

questions.q: How did the mother feel when she received the gift?
questions.exp: She was "so moved that she hugged them both and wept with joy."

questions.q: The word "savings" in the passage means:
questions.exp: "Savings" refers to money that has been kept over time rather than spent.

questions.q: The word "wept" in the passage means:
questions.exp: "Wept" is the past tense of "weep" — to cry.

questions.q: Choose the correct form: "She ______ her homework last night."
questions.exp: "Did" is the simple past tense of "do" — used for a completed action.

questions.q: The plural of "knife" is:
questions.exp: Words ending in "-fe" form their plural by changing "fe" to "ves": knife → kn...

questions.q: Which word is a PRONOUN?
questions.exp: "She" is a pronoun — it replaces a noun.

questions.q: Choose the sentence with correct CAPITALISATION:
questions.exp: Only the first word of a sentence and proper nouns are capitalised: "My name ...

questions.q: "The dog barked loudly." The word "loudly" is a(n):
questions.exp: "Loudly" tells us HOW the dog barked — it is an adverb.

questions.q: Which is the SYNONYM of "brave"?
questions.exp: "Brave" and "fearless" both mean not afraid — they are synonyms.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
questions.exp: "We don't know" is correct. "Don't" (do not) is used with I/you/we/they.

questions.q: What is the SUPERLATIVE form of "good"?
questions.exp: "Good" has irregular comparative "better" and superlative "best."

questions.q: Choose the correct preposition: "The book is ______ the shelf."
questions.exp: "On" is used when something rests on a surface.

questions.q: The word "unhappy" contains the prefix ______ which means NOT.
questions.exp: The prefix "un-" is added before the adjective to mean "not": un- + happy = u...

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Perceive" is correctly spelt. "i before e except after c" — after "c," use "...

questions.q: Which sentence is in the FUTURE TENSE?
questions.exp: "Will eat" indicates a future action.

questions.q: The opposite of "generous" is:
questions.exp: "Generous" means willing to give. The opposite is "selfish."

questions.q: Choose the word that fits: "She spoke in a very ______ voice."
questions.exp: "Loud" is the adjective form — it correctly describes the noun "voice."

questions.q: Which of these is a PROPER NOUN?
questions.exp: "Freetown" is a proper noun — a specific place name. It is always capitalised.

questions.q: The sentence "Sit down!" is:
questions.exp: An imperative sentence gives a command: "Sit down!" is a command.

questions.q: Choose the correct word: "There are ______ pupils in the class."
questions.exp: "Few" is used with countable nouns (pupils can be counted).

questions.q: What is the PAST TENSE of "write"?
questions.exp: "Write" has the irregular past tense "wrote."

questions.q: Choose the sentence with the CORRECT use of apostrophe:
questions.exp: "The dog's bone" correctly uses an apostrophe to show possession.

questions.q: Which word completes: "Aminata is ______ than Mariama."
questions.exp: When comparing two people, use the comparative: tall → taller.

questions.q: The word "carefully" is what part of speech?
questions.exp: "Carefully" describes HOW something is done — it is an adverb.

questions.q: Choose the SYNONYM of "begin":
questions.exp: "Begin" and "start" have the same meaning — they are synonyms.

questions.q: Which sentence uses "there", "their" and "they're" CORRECTLY?
questions.exp: "They're" = they are; "their" = belonging to them; "there" = a place. Correc...

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
questions.exp: "He has eaten his food" is correct — "has eaten" is the correct present perfe...

questions.q: The word "geography" has ______ syllables.
questions.exp: "Ge-og-ra-phy" has four syllables.

questions.q: Choose the word that means "to look at something carefully":
questions.exp: "Examine" means to inspect or look at something carefully.

questions.q: Which of these words has a SILENT letter?
questions.exp: "Knife" has a silent "k."

questions.q: The sentence "How wonderful she sings!" is:
questions.exp: An exclamatory sentence expresses strong emotion ending with "!".

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Occasion" is correctly spelt — double "c", single "s."

questions.q: What does the suffix "-tion" do to the word "educate"?
questions.exp: Adding "-tion" to "educate" forms "education" — a noun.

questions.q: Choose the word that BEST fills: "The thief ran ______ when he heard the police."
questions.exp: "Slowly" is an adverb — it tells us HOW the thief ran.

questions.q: Which word is an ADJECTIVE in: "The clever student passed the exam"?
questions.exp: "Clever" describes the noun "student" — it is an adjective.

questions.q: The word "impossible" contains which prefix?
questions.exp: "Im-" is the prefix meaning "not" in "impossible."

questions.q: Choose the ANTONYM of "victory":
questions.exp: The antonym of "victory" is "defeat."

questions.q: Which of the following is a COLLECTIVE NOUN?
questions.exp: "Flock" is a collective noun — it refers to a group of birds or sheep.

questions.q: Choose the correct form: "By next year, she ______ Class 6."
questions.exp: "Will have finished" is the future perfect tense.

questions.q: Which sentence contains a SIMILE?
questions.exp: A simile compares two things using "like" or "as." "He ran like the wind."

questions.q: Choose the word that means "very tired":
questions.exp: "Exhausted" means extremely tired.

questions.q: The MAIN IDEA of the passage about Fatima and Karim is:
o: Two children showed love and appreciation for their mother
questions.exp: The passage centres on their loving act of buying their mother a birthday gift.

questions.q: In a letter, which part comes IMMEDIATELY after the date?
questions.exp: The structure of a letter: Address → Date → Salutation → Body → Closing → Sig...

2022.subject: English Language
2022.instructions:
This paper contains 50 questions. Answer ALL questions. Choose the best answer from the options A–E. Questions 1–10 are based on the passage below.

PASSAGE: The town of Makeni is the capital of the Northern Province of Sierra Leone. It is one of the largest cities in the country. People in Makeni are known for their friendliness and hospitality. The town has a busy market where traders sell food, clothing, household goods and farm produce. Many schools, hospitals and mosques can be found in Makeni. Young people from Makeni have gone on to become doctors, engineers, teachers and government workers. The town is connected to Freetown and other cities by well-maintained roads.

questions.q: What is Makeni the capital of?
questions.exp: The passage states Makeni "is the capital of the Northern Province of Sierra ...

questions.q: What are people in Makeni known for?
questions.exp: The passage says "People in Makeni are known for their friendliness and hospi...

questions.q: What is sold at the busy market?
o: Food, clothing, household goods and farm produce
questions.exp: The passage says traders sell "food, clothing, household goods and farm produ...

questions.q: Which of these is NOT mentioned as a building in Makeni?
questions.exp: The passage mentions schools, hospitals and mosques — hotels are NOT mentioned.

questions.q: What have young people from Makeni become?
o: Doctors, engineers, teachers and government workers
questions.exp: The passage lists: "doctors, engineers, teachers and government workers."

questions.q: How is Makeni connected to other cities?
questions.exp: The passage says it is connected "by well-maintained roads."

questions.q: The word "hospitality" in the passage means:
o: Welcoming and being kind to guests
questions.exp: "Hospitality" means being welcoming, warm and generous to visitors.

questions.q: Which province is Makeni in?
questions.exp: The passage clearly states Makeni is in the Northern Province.

questions.q: Which word from the passage means "products grown on a farm"?
questions.exp: "Farm produce" refers to food and other products grown on farms.

questions.q: The TONE of this passage is:
questions.exp: The passage gives factual information about Makeni in a positive, informative...

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Definitely" is the correct spelling.

questions.q: "The girl who won the prize is my sister." This sentence contains a:
questions.exp: "Who won the prize" is a relative clause — it gives more information about "t...

questions.q: The word "beautiful" is what type of word?
questions.exp: "Beautiful" describes a noun — it is an adjective.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT plural: "The women carried ______ on their heads."
questions.exp: The regular plural of "basket" is "baskets."

questions.q: Which word is a CONJUNCTION in: "She was tired but she kept walking."?
questions.exp: "But" is a conjunction — it joins two contrasting ideas.

questions.q: Choose the sentence in the PASSIVE VOICE:
o: The letter was written by her.
questions.exp: "The letter was written by her" — passive voice. The subject receives the act...

questions.q: Which sentence is INTERROGATIVE?
questions.exp: An interrogative sentence asks a question: "Is she a teacher?"

questions.q: Choose the ANTONYM of "victory":
questions.exp: "Defeat" is the opposite of "victory."

questions.q: "She has been studying since morning." This sentence is in:
questions.exp: "Has been studying" is the present perfect continuous.

questions.q: Which word contains a SILENT letter?
questions.exp: "Lamb" has a silent "b."

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
questions.exp: "We are going to school" is correct.

questions.q: The word "government" has ______ syllables.
questions.exp: "Gov-ern-ment" has three syllables.

questions.q: Which of these is a COMMON NOUN?
questions.exp: "Girl" is a common noun — a general word, not a specific name.

questions.q: Choose the word that means "to say sorry":
questions.exp: "Apologise" means to say sorry or express regret.

questions.q: The suffix "-ness" in "darkness" means:
questions.exp: "-ness" turns an adjective into a noun expressing a state or quality: dark → ...

questions.q: Choose the correct COMPARATIVE form: "This road is ______ than that one."
questions.exp: "Bad" has the irregular comparative "worse."

questions.q: Which word is a PREPOSITION in: "The cat sat under the table"?
questions.exp: "Under" shows the relationship between the cat and the table — it is a prepos...

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Separate" is correct — sep-a-rate.

questions.q: "Even though it was raining, she went to school." This shows:
questions.exp: "Even though" introduces a contrast.

questions.q: The word "international" contains "inter-" which means:
questions.exp: The prefix "inter-" means "between" or "among."

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
o: Neither John nor James are coming.
Neither John nor James is coming.
Neither John nor James were coming.
Neither John nor James have come.
Neither John nor James was coming.
questions.exp: With "neither...nor," the verb agrees with the nearest subject: "James is" — ...

questions.q: "She arrived before the bell rang." Which word is a CONJUNCTION?
questions.exp: "Before" connects two clauses — it is a subordinating conjunction.

questions.q: Which word means "very happy"?
questions.exp: "Elated" means extremely happy or joyful.

questions.q: What is the COMPARATIVE form of "far"?
questions.exp: "Far" has irregular forms: comparative = farther.

questions.q: Choose the word that BEST fills: "Please ______ the door when you leave."
questions.exp: "Close" is the correct imperative (command) form.

questions.q: Which sentence uses DIRECT SPEECH correctly?
questions.exp: Direct speech uses quotation marks for the speaker's exact words.

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Accommodation" is correct — double "c" and double "m."

questions.q: What does the word "perseverance" mean?
questions.exp: "Perseverance" means continuing to do something despite difficulties.

questions.q: Which sentence uses a METAPHOR?
o: She is as tall as a tree.
questions.exp: A metaphor directly says one thing IS another. "Life is a journey."

questions.q: Choose the SYNONYM of "ancient":
questions.exp: "Ancient" means very old — "old" is its synonym.

questions.q: "She cleaned the house quickly." Change to PASSIVE VOICE:
o: The house was cleaned quickly by her.
questions.exp: Passive: "The house was cleaned quickly by her."

questions.q: Which of these is an ABSTRACT NOUN?
questions.exp: "Courage" cannot be seen or touched — it is an abstract noun.

questions.q: Choose the word that BEST fills: "He was so weak that he could ______ walk."
questions.exp: "Hardly" means almost not at all — "he could hardly walk."

questions.q: Which tense is used in: "By the time she arrived, he had left."?
questions.exp: "Had left" is the past perfect — for an action completed before another past ...

questions.q: The word "unhealthy" has ______ syllables.
questions.exp: "Un-heal-thy" has three syllables.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
o: Each of the boys have a book.
Each of the boys has a book.
Each of the boys are having a book.
Each of the boys were having a book.
Each of the boys had been have a book.
questions.exp: "Each" is singular — "Each of the boys has a book."

questions.q: Which of the following is a SIMILE?
o: The moon is a silver coin.
questions.exp: A simile uses "like" or "as": "She sings like an angel."

questions.q: In a formal letter, how should you close if you began with "Dear Sir"?
questions.exp: When you do not know the recipient (Dear Sir/Madam), close with "Yours faithf...

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
o: She has ate all the food.
She have eaten all the food.
She has eaten all the food.
She had ate all the food.
She have ate all the food.
questions.exp: "She has eaten" — present perfect: has + past participle (eaten, not ate).

questions.q: Which of these is a COMPOUND SENTENCE?
o: She is tall and he is short.
questions.exp: A compound sentence joins two independent clauses with a conjunction.

2023.subject: English Language
2023.instructions:
This paper contains 50 questions. Answer ALL questions. Choose the best answer from the options A–E. Questions 1–10 are based on the passage below.

PASSAGE: Ibrahim is a farmer who lives in a village outside Bo. He grows rice, cassava and groundnuts on his farm. Every day, Ibrahim wakes up early and walks to his farm with his tools. He works from morning until afternoon, taking only a short break for lunch. His wife, Adama, cooks the meals and helps with the harvest during the dry season. Ibrahim sells some of his crops at the Bo market and uses the money to send his three children to school. He believes education is the most important gift a parent can give a child.

questions.q: Where does Ibrahim live?
questions.exp: The passage says Ibrahim "lives in a village outside Bo."

questions.q: Which crops does Ibrahim grow?
questions.exp: The passage lists: "rice, cassava and groundnuts."

questions.q: What does Ibrahim take to his farm every day?
questions.exp: The passage says he "walks to his farm with his tools."

questions.q: When does Ibrahim work on his farm?
questions.exp: "He works from morning until afternoon."

questions.q: What does Adama (Ibrahim's wife) do?
o: She sells food in the market
She cooks meals and helps with the harvest
questions.exp: "His wife, Adama, cooks the meals and helps with the harvest during the dry s...

questions.q: Where does Ibrahim sell his crops?
questions.exp: "Ibrahim sells some of his crops at the Bo market."

questions.q: Why does Ibrahim sell his crops?
o: To send his children to school
questions.exp: He "uses the money to send his three children to school."

questions.q: How many children does Ibrahim have?
questions.exp: "His three children" — Ibrahim has three children.

questions.q: The word "harvest" in the passage means:
questions.exp: "Harvest" is the process of gathering crops that are ready.

questions.q: What does Ibrahim believe is the most important gift for a child?
questions.exp: "He believes education is the most important gift a parent can give a child."

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Knowledge" is correctly spelt — silent "k" and the "-dge" ending.

questions.q: Which sentence is in the SIMPLE PAST TENSE?
questions.exp: "Played" is in the simple past tense.

questions.q: Which of these words is a VERB?
questions.exp: "Construct" is a verb — it describes an action (to build).

questions.q: The plural of "ox" is:
questions.exp: "Ox" has the irregular plural "oxen."

questions.q: "The children were singing when I arrived." This sentence uses:
questions.exp: "Were singing" is the past continuous.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
o: None of the students have finished.
None of the students has finished.
None of the student has finished.
None of the students had finish.
None of the student have finished.
questions.exp: "None" is treated as singular: "None of the students has finished."

questions.q: The word "international" belongs to which word class?
questions.exp: "International" describes a noun — it is an adjective.

questions.q: Which sentence contains a METAPHOR?
o: He is as strong as a bull.
questions.exp: "The children are busy bees" is a metaphor — directly comparing children to b...

questions.q: The suffix "-ful" in "hopeful" means:
questions.exp: "-ful" means "full of" or "having the quality of." Hopeful = full of hope.

questions.q: Choose the ANTONYM of "generous":
questions.exp: "Selfish" is the opposite of "generous."

questions.q: Which of these is an ABSTRACT NOUN?
questions.exp: "Patience" is an abstract noun — a quality you cannot touch or see.

questions.q: Choose the word that BEST completes: "She is ______ at Mathematics than her brother."
questions.exp: Comparing two people uses the comparative: good → better.

questions.q: Which word contains a PREFIX meaning "before"?
questions.exp: "Pre-" means "before." Preview = to view before.

questions.q: Choose the correct ARTICLE: "She is ______ honest person."
questions.exp: "Honest" starts with a silent "h" (sounds like "onest") — use "an."

questions.q: Which sentence uses a SIMILE?
o: She is as brave as a lion.
questions.exp: A simile uses "as...as" or "like." "She is as brave as a lion."

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Occurrence" is correctly spelt — double "c" and double "r."

questions.q: Which is the SUPERLATIVE form of "bad"?
questions.exp: "Bad" → worse → worst.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT use of "who" or "whom": "To ______ should I send this letter?"
questions.exp: "Whom" is used as an object pronoun. "To whom" — whom receives the action.

questions.q: "Unless you study, you will fail." This sentence shows:
questions.exp: "Unless" introduces a condition.

questions.q: The word "happiness" is formed from "happy" by:
questions.exp: Adding "-ness" to the adjective "happy" creates the noun "happiness."

questions.q: Choose the sentence in REPORTED SPEECH:
o: She said that she was tired.
questions.exp: Reported speech: "She said that she was tired" — no quotation marks, tenses s...

questions.q: Which word means "relating to the sea"?
questions.exp: "Marine" relates to the sea.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
o: Either the boys or the girl are late.
Either the boys or the girl is late.
Either the boys or the girl were late.
Either the boys or the girl am late.
Either the boys or the girl have been late.
questions.exp: With "either...or," the verb agrees with the nearest subject: "the girl is" —...

questions.q: "She is the most intelligent student in the class." The word "most intelligent" is:
questions.exp: "Most intelligent" is the superlative degree.

questions.q: Which sentence uses the APOSTROPHE correctly?
o: The boys' books are on the table.
questions.exp: "The boys' books" correctly uses apostrophe for plural possession.

questions.q: Choose the word that means "to make something better":
questions.exp: "Improve" means to make something better.

questions.q: Which of these is a CONJUNCTION?
questions.exp: "Although" is a conjunction — it connects two clauses showing contrast.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
questions.exp: "She has gone home" is the correct present perfect.

questions.q: The word "bicycle" has ______ syllables.
questions.exp: "Bi-cy-cle" has three syllables.

questions.q: Which sentence contains a PERSONIFICATION?
o: She is as fast as a cheetah.
The car is like a rocket.
The wind whispered through the trees.
questions.exp: Personification gives human qualities to non-human things: "The wind whispered."

questions.q: Choose the word that BEST fills: "He worked very hard; ______, he failed the exam."
questions.exp: "However" shows contrast — despite working hard, the unexpected result was fa...

questions.q: The word "transport" in "They transport goods by road" is a:
questions.exp: In "they transport goods," "transport" is the action being done — it is a verb.

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Deceive" is correctly spelt. After "c," use "ei": deceive, receive.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT use of tense: "She ______ dinner when the guests arrived."
questions.exp: "Was cooking" is the past continuous — an action in progress when another hap...

questions.q: "The teacher asked the pupils to be quiet." This is an example of:
questions.exp: Reported speech conveys what was said without using the speaker's exact words.

questions.q: Which of these sentences is DECLARATIVE?
questions.exp: A declarative sentence makes a statement: "She is a teacher."

questions.q: In a formal letter, the address of the writer is placed:
questions.exp: In a formal letter, the sender's address is written at the top right corner.

questions.q: Which sentence uses the PRESENT PERFECT correctly?
questions.exp: "They have finished the work" is correct present perfect.

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Independence" is correctly spelt — ending in "-ence."

questions.q: In an essay, the THESIS STATEMENT is found in the:
questions.exp: The thesis statement appears in the introduction.

2024.subject: English Language
2024.instructions:
This paper contains 50 questions. Answer ALL questions. Choose the best answer from the options A–E. Questions 1–10 are based on the passage below.

PASSAGE: Kossoh Town is a growing community located in the New London area of Sierra Leone. It has several primary and secondary schools, a health clinic and a busy market. The people of Kossoh Town are hardworking and their children are known to perform well in national examinations. Community leaders work with parents and teachers to support education. One proud institution is Wisdom Academy International, a daycare, nursery and primary school that is building the leaders of tomorrow. The school encourages pupils to think creatively, work hard and develop strong values.

questions.q: Where is Kossoh Town located?
questions.exp: The passage says Kossoh Town is "located in the New London area of Sierra Leo...

questions.q: What facilities does Kossoh Town have?
o: Schools, a health clinic and a market
questions.exp: The passage mentions "several primary and secondary schools, a health clinic ...

questions.q: What are the children of Kossoh Town known for?
questions.exp: Children "are known to perform well in national examinations."

questions.q: Who works together to support education?
questions.exp: "Community leaders work with parents and teachers to support education."

questions.q: What type of school is Wisdom Academy International?
questions.exp: The passage describes it as "a daycare, nursery and primary school."

questions.q: What does Wisdom Academy aim to build?
questions.exp: "Building the leaders of tomorrow."

questions.q: Which THREE things does the school encourage?
o: Thinking creatively, working hard and developing strong values
questions.exp: "Think creatively, work hard and develop strong values."

questions.q: The word "institution" in the passage refers to:
questions.exp: An "institution" is an established organisation.

questions.q: The word "creatively" in the passage is a(n):
questions.exp: "Creatively" describes HOW pupils are encouraged to think — it is an adverb.

questions.q: The MAIN MESSAGE of this passage is:
o: Kossoh Town has a big market
Education and community support help children succeed
questions.exp: The passage emphasises how community support and schools help children succeed.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECTLY spelt word:
questions.exp: "Privilege" is correctly spelt.

questions.q: What is the SUPERLATIVE form of "many"?
questions.exp: "Many" → more → most.

questions.q: The word "kindness" is formed from "kind" by:
questions.exp: Adding "-ness" to "kind" creates the noun "kindness."

questions.q: Which of these sentences contains an ERROR?
questions.exp: "We was happy" is incorrect — should be "We were happy."

questions.q: Choose the word that BEST fills: "She is ______ interested in science."
questions.exp: "Deeply" is an adverb modifying the adjective "interested."

questions.q: Which of these is a COLLECTIVE NOUN?
questions.exp: "Herd" is a collective noun — a group of cattle or animals.

questions.q: Choose the ANTONYM of "abundant":
questions.exp: "Abundant" means a lot. Its antonym is "scarce."

questions.q: The word "preview" contains the prefix "pre-" which means:
questions.exp: "Pre-" means "before." A preview = something seen BEFORE the main event.

questions.q: Which sentence is in the PASSIVE VOICE?
o: The report was written by her.
questions.exp: "The report was written by her" is passive voice.

questions.q: Choose the word with the CORRECT spelling:
questions.exp: "Excellent" is correctly spelt — double "l."

questions.q: Which sentence uses REPORTED SPEECH correctly?
o: She said that she is tired.
She said that she was tired.
She said she is being tired.
questions.exp: In reported speech, tenses shift: "am" becomes "was."

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
questions.exp: "News" is uncountable and takes a singular verb: "The news is shocking."

questions.q: Which word means "very important or crucial"?
questions.exp: "Essential" means absolutely necessary.

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Calendar" is correctly spelt — cal-en-dar.

questions.q: Which sentence uses ALLITERATION?
questions.exp: Alliteration = repetition of the same starting sound. "Peter Piper Picked" — ...

questions.q: The word "autobiography" means:
o: A book written by someone about their own life
questions.exp: "Auto-" = self, "bio-" = life, "-graphy" = writing. Autobiography = a person...

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT verb form: "He ______ to the market every Friday."
questions.exp: Simple present with third-person singular (he) requires "goes."

questions.q: Which sentence has the CORRECT use of "its" and "it's"?
questions.exp: "It's" = it is. "It's a beautiful day" = "It is a beautiful day."

questions.q: Choose the SYNONYM of "sufficient":
questions.exp: "Sufficient" means as much as needed — "enough" is its synonym.

questions.q: The sentence "What a terrible storm!" is:
questions.exp: An exclamatory sentence expresses strong emotion ending with "!".

questions.q: Which word is a PREPOSITION in: "She walked through the forest"?
questions.exp: "Through" shows the relationship between "she" and "the forest."

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT comparative: "Gold is ______ than silver."
questions.exp: Long adjectives use "more" + adjective for comparative: "more valuable."

questions.q: What is the PAST TENSE of "teach"?
questions.exp: "Teach" has the irregular past tense "taught."

questions.q: Which sentence contains a CONJUNCTION?
questions.exp: "But" is a conjunction joining two contrasting clauses.

questions.q: Choose the word that means "a strong feeling of dislike":
questions.exp: "Hatred" means an intense feeling of strong dislike.

questions.q: The word "unbelievable" has ______ syllables.
questions.exp: "Un-be-liev-a-ble" has five syllables.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
o: She has ate all the food.
She have eaten all the food.
She has eaten all the food.
She had ate all the food.
She have ate all the food.
questions.exp: "She has eaten all the food" — has + past participle (eaten, not ate).

questions.q: Which of these is a COMPOUND SENTENCE?
o: She is tall and he is short.
questions.exp: A compound sentence joins two independent clauses with a conjunction.

questions.q: Choose the word that BEST fills: "She is fluent ______ three languages."
questions.exp: "Fluent in" is the correct collocation.

questions.q: Which sentence uses HYPERBOLE?
o: I have told you a million times!
questions.exp: Hyperbole is deliberate exaggeration: "a million times."

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Embarrass" is correctly spelt — double "r" and double "s."

questions.q: Which word means "to walk in a proud or confident manner"?
questions.exp: "Stride" means to walk with long, confident steps.

questions.q: The word "democracy" has ______ syllables.
questions.exp: "De-moc-ra-cy" has four syllables.

questions.q: "Despite being tired, she finished the work." What does "despite" show?
questions.exp: "Despite" introduces a contrast.

questions.q: Which type of writing tells the story of your own life?
questions.exp: An autobiography is a book or account written by someone about their own life.

questions.q: In an essay, the THESIS STATEMENT is found in the:
questions.exp: The thesis statement appears in the introduction.

questions.q: A word that sounds the same as another but has a different meaning is called a:
questions.exp: A homophone sounds the same but has a different spelling/meaning — e.g. "ther...

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Acquaintance" is correctly spelt — note the "cqu" cluster.

questions.q: Which literary device is used in: "The mountains sing and the rivers laugh"?
questions.exp: Giving human qualities (singing, laughing) to non-human things is personifica...

questions.q: In composition writing, which of these makes a STRONG opening sentence?
o: As the sun rose over the hills, a new adventure began.
I will write about the market.
questions.exp: A strong opening engages the reader immediately using imagery.

2025.subject: English Language
2025.instructions:
This paper contains 50 questions. Answer ALL questions. Choose the best answer from the options A–E. Questions 1–10 are based on the passage below.

PASSAGE: Sierra Leone is a small country on the west coast of Africa. It is bordered by Guinea to the north and east, and Liberia to the south-east. The Atlantic Ocean lies to the west. The country gained independence on 27th April 1961. Freetown is the capital and the largest city. Sierra Leone is rich in natural resources including diamonds, gold, rutile and iron ore. The country is also known for its beautiful beaches, rainforests and friendly people. Agriculture employs most of the population. English is the official language, while Krio is widely spoken as a common language across ethnic groups.

questions.q: Where is Sierra Leone located?
questions.exp: The passage states Sierra Leone is "on the west coast of Africa."

questions.q: Which country borders Sierra Leone to the south-east?
questions.exp: "Liberia to the south-east."

questions.q: When did Sierra Leone gain independence?
questions.exp: "The country gained independence on 27th April 1961."

questions.q: What is the capital of Sierra Leone?
questions.exp: "Freetown is the capital and the largest city."

questions.q: Which of these is NOT listed as a natural resource of Sierra Leone?
questions.exp: The passage lists diamonds, gold, rutile and iron ore. Crude oil is NOT menti...

questions.q: What is the official language of Sierra Leone?
questions.exp: "English is the official language."

questions.q: The word "bordered" in the passage means:
questions.exp: "Bordered by" means to share a boundary with another country.

questions.q: What does agriculture provide for most Sierra Leoneans?
questions.exp: "Agriculture employs most of the population" — it provides employment.

questions.q: The word "ethnic" in the passage refers to:
o: Groups sharing the same culture or origin
questions.exp: "Ethnic groups" refers to groups sharing the same cultural background or origin.

questions.q: Which ocean lies to the west of Sierra Leone?
questions.exp: "The Atlantic Ocean lies to the west."

questions.q: Choose the CORRECTLY spelt word:
questions.exp: "Necessary" is correctly spelt — one "c" and double "s."

questions.q: Which word is an ADVERB in: "She spoke very softly"?
questions.exp: Both "very" (modifying "softly") and "softly" (modifying "spoke") are adverbs.

questions.q: The plural of "datum" is:
questions.exp: "Datum" has the Latin plural "data."

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
questions.exp: "Information" is uncountable and takes a singular verb.

questions.q: Which word means "to make something worse"?
questions.exp: "Aggravate" means to make a problem or situation worse.

questions.q: Choose the word that BEST fills: "The results ______ announced tomorrow."
questions.exp: "Will be announced" is the future passive voice.

questions.q: The word "independence" contains which suffix?
questions.exp: "Independence" ends in "-ence" — a noun-forming suffix.

questions.q: Choose the ANTONYM of "restrict":
questions.exp: "Restrict" means to limit. Its antonym is "liberate."

questions.q: Which sentence uses the PRESENT PERFECT correctly?
questions.exp: "They have finished the work" is correct present perfect.

questions.q: "The teacher, as well as the pupils, ______ present." Choose the correct verb.
questions.exp: With "as well as," the verb agrees with the FIRST (main) subject: "The teache...

questions.q: Which sentence contains ONOMATOPOEIA?
questions.exp: "Buzzed" is a word that sounds like the noise it describes — onomatopoeia.

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Independence" is correctly spelt — ending in "-ence."

questions.q: The word "bicycle" comes from:
questions.exp: "Bi-" means two and "cycle" means wheel — a bicycle has two wheels.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
questions.exp: "She has been waiting" is the present perfect continuous.

questions.q: Which word means "relating to a city or town"?
questions.exp: "Urban" means relating to a city or town.

questions.q: Choose the SYNONYM of "courageous":
questions.exp: "Courageous" and "brave" both mean having courage.

questions.q: "He is the boy whose father is a doctor." The word "whose" is a:
questions.exp: "Whose" is a relative pronoun introducing a relative clause.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT use of "less" or "fewer": "There are ______ pupils today."
questions.exp: "Fewer" is used with countable nouns (pupils can be counted).

questions.q: Which word contains a SUFFIX that means "one who"?
questions.exp: "-er" in "teacher" means "one who teaches."

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Committee" is correctly spelt — double "m," double "t," double "e."

questions.q: Which of these is a COMPLEX SENTENCE?
o: She studied hard because she wanted to pass.
questions.exp: A complex sentence has one independent clause and one dependent clause.

questions.q: The word "revise" means:
o: To see something for the first time
questions.exp: "Revise" means to review material again — especially studying before an exam.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
o: He is more cleverer than her.
She is most tallest in the class.
He is more intelligent than her.
She is more taller than him.
questions.exp: "More intelligent" is the correct comparative for long adjectives.

questions.q: A word that sounds the same as another but has a different meaning is called a:
questions.exp: A homophone sounds the same but has a different spelling/meaning.

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.exp: "Acquaintance" is correctly spelt — note the "cqu" cluster.

questions.q: "She neither ate nor drank." This sentence is:
questions.exp: "Neither...nor" makes the sentence negative.

questions.q: Which sentence uses the CORRECT form of the verb?
o: She suggested to go to market.
questions.exp: "Suggest" is followed by a gerund (-ing form): "She suggested going."

questions.q: Choose the word that means "a person who moves to another country to live permanently":
questions.exp: An "immigrant" is someone who comes to live permanently in a new country.

questions.q: The word "extraordinary" has ______ syllables.
questions.exp: "Ex-tra-or-di-na-ry" — five syllables in standard British English pronunciation.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT sentence:
o: He is use to waking up early.
He is used to waking up early.
He is used to wake up early.
He use to wake up early.
He used to waking up early.
questions.exp: "Used to" + gerund = accustomed to: "He is used to waking up early."

questions.q: A BIOGRAPHY is:
o: A book written by someone about their own life
A story about a made-up person
A book written about someone else's life
questions.exp: A biography is written ABOUT someone's life by ANOTHER person.

questions.q: Which word means "too proud of oneself"?
questions.exp: "Arrogant" means having an exaggerated sense of one's own importance.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECTLY punctuated sentence:
questions.exp: Direct speech: comma after "said," then quote in inverted commas.

questions.q: What is the GERUND form of the verb "swim"?
questions.exp: A gerund is the -ing form of a verb used as a noun: "swimming."

questions.q: Choose the word that BEST fills: "The government should ______ corruption."
questions.exp: "Combat" means to fight against — the government should fight corruption.

questions.q: Which literary device is used in: "The mountains sing and the rivers laugh"?
questions.exp: Giving human qualities to non-human things is personification.

questions.q: Choose the CORRECT form: "By the time you arrive, I ______ the food."
questions.exp: "Will have cooked" is the future perfect — completed before a future point.

questions.q: The word "multicultural" — "Multi-" means:
questions.exp: "Multi-" is a prefix meaning many — multicultural = involving many cultures.

questions.q: Choose the ANTONYM of "transparent":
questions.exp: "Transparent" = you can see through it. Opposite = "opaque."

questions.q: In composition writing, which of these makes a STRONG opening sentence?
o: As the sun rose over the hills, a new adventure began.
I will write about the market.
questions.exp: A strong opening uses imagery and creates interest immediately.

2026.subject: English Language
2026.instructions:
Answer all the questions. Each question is followed by four options lettered A to D. Find the correct option for each question. Shade in pencil on your answer sheet the answer space which bears the same letter as the option you have chosen. Give only one answer to each question. (NPSE 04 — English I, Objective Test, May 2026, West African Examinations Council)

SECTION A — Read Passages I and II carefully and answer the questions which follow.

PASSAGE I: "The Truth Shall Set You Free" was the most popular sermon of Reverend Desmond Jones of God's Bible Church in Boli Town. In this sermon, he would preach about the importance of integrity and honesty in one's way of life. He would always stress that no matter the condition, one must learn to call a spade a spade. For this reason, Reverend Jones was the most popular man in his community. However, not everybody was in favour of the reverend. The young people, especially those involved in petty theft and gambling, saw him as a threat to their existence because he would openly criticize them in his sermons. So, they planned to kidnap him. One day, a group of young men armed with stones, machetes and knives invaded the church in order to capture Reverend Jones. It happened at a time when the reverend was having his Wednesday Special Service at the upper hall of the church. These young men entered the church and met the cleaner. He was cleaning the basement. He was shocked and terrified to see a group of young men armed to the teeth. They beat him up and threatened to kill him if he failed to tell them the whereabouts of the reverend. The cleaner lied that the reverend was resting at the back of the church. Without hesitation, the youths rushed to the back of the church but the reverend was not there. Meanwhile, the cleaner ran as fast as he could to the upper hall of the church to inform the reverend about the incident. When the reverend saw the cleaner in a state of shock and nervousness, he immediately paused and enquired what the problem was. The cleaner explained all that had happened and advised the reverend to run away. The reverend told the cleaner that he would not go anywhere, but remain under God's protection, because of his faith in God.

PASSAGE II: Musa's father, Mr Brima, lived in Bongohun, a small village in the North-East of Sierra Leone. His father had two wives; each gave birth to two boys and a girl. Musa's mother died just after giving birth to him. The sudden death of Fatmata, Musa's mother, made Mr Brima marry two more wives at once. Unfortunately, when the new brides arrived at Mr Brima's house, they were not happy to see Musa. They immediately asked their husband to send Musa out of the house. On hearing this, Mr Brima was disappointed at their words. He called the two wives, Salamatu and Aminata, and told them to leave his house immediately if they did not want to see Musa. Their children who were born to other men apologized to Mr Brima on behalf of their mothers and pleaded with him to allow their mothers to stay. Mr Brima accepted and they all lived happily and peacefully.

questions.q: What is the name of the reverend mentioned in the passage?
questions.exp: The passage states the sermon belonged to "Reverend Desmond Jones of God's B...

questions.q: The most popular sermon of the reverend was
o: The Truth Will Sometimes Set You Free
The Truth Shall Set You Free
The Truth is Setting You Free
questions.exp: The passage states: "The Truth Shall Set You Free" was the most popular sermo...

questions.q: Who did the young men meet at the basement of the church?
questions.exp: The passage says the young men entered the church and "met the cleaner. He wa...

questions.q: Which of the following weapons did the young men bring to harm the reverend?
questions.exp: The passage states they were "armed with stones, machetes and knives." Answer A.

questions.q: To "call a spade a spade" in the passage means to
questions.exp: "Call a spade a spade" is an idiom meaning to speak plainly and honestly — to...

questions.q: The reverend's life was saved because
o: he put his faith in God
his cleaner called a spade a spade
his cleaner lied that he ran away
questions.exp: The reverend said he would "remain under God's protection, because of his fa...

questions.q: How did the young men feel when they met the reverend?
questions.exp: The passage says "They were all filled with fear and were trembling upon seei...

questions.q: What is the name of Musa's father?
questions.exp: The passage states "Musa's father, Mr Brima." Answer C.

questions.q: How many wives did Musa's father marry in all?
questions.exp: He had two wives originally; after Musa's mother died, he married two more. ...

questions.q: How many biological children did Musa's father have?
questions.exp: Each of the two original wives gave birth to two boys and a girl = 3 children...

questions.q: The two wives Musa's father later married were
questions.exp: The passage names the two new wives as "Salamatu and Aminata." Answer D.

questions.q: What did Mr Brima do when he noticed that his two new wives did not like Musa?
o: He told them to leave his house
He sent their sons from his house
questions.exp: He "told them to leave his house immediately if they did not want to see Musa...

questions.q: What happened to Musa's mother?
questions.exp: "Musa's mother died just after giving birth to him." Answer A.

questions.q: Bongohun is found in the .......... of Sierra Leone.
questions.exp: The passage states Bongohun is "a small village in the North-East of Sierra L...

questions.q: SECTION B — When the teacher announced that all the pupils did well in the test, they were all on cloud nine. This means that the pupils were
questions.exp: "On cloud nine" is an idiom meaning extremely happy or excited. Answer C.

questions.q: As it was getting to 9:00 P.M., mother told my younger sister to hit the sack. This means that my mother told my sister to go
questions.exp: "Hit the sack" is an idiom meaning to go to bed/sleep. Answer C.

questions.q: SECTION C — The pastor told the man to forgive his son. (Synonym for "forgive")
questions.exp: "Forgive" means to stop being angry and excuse someone. "Pardon" is its synon...

questions.q: The principal awarded certificates during the prize giving ceremony. (Synonym for "awarded")
questions.exp: "Awarded" means given as a prize or recognition. "Gave" is the closest synony...

questions.q: Most pupils blame teachers for their failure in examinations. (Synonym for "blame")
questions.exp: To "blame" someone is to hold them responsible. "Abuse" in this context means...

questions.q: SECTION D — My sister .......... French every day. (Correct tense)
questions.exp: Simple present tense with third-person singular "sister" requires "speaks." A...

questions.q: They went to the shop yesterday, but they didn't .......... me.
questions.exp: After "didn't," the base form of the verb is used: "didn't see." Answer B.

questions.q: John .......... a circle in the sand yesterday.
questions.exp: "Yesterday" indicates past tense. The past tense of "draw" is "drew." Answer A.

questions.q: It always .......... in July every year.
questions.exp: A habitual/general truth uses simple present tense: "rains." Answer C.

questions.q: We .......... our sister this Sunday.
questions.exp: "This Sunday" refers to the future. "Shall visit" is the correct future tense...

questions.q: SECTION E — My teacher has just received a letter .......... his brother.
questions.exp: "Received a letter from" — "from" shows the source of the letter. Answer B.

questions.q: It is good to attend meetings .......... time.
questions.exp: "On time" means punctually. Answer A.

questions.q: It is not advisable to jump .......... a fence.
questions.exp: "Jump over" a fence is the correct collocation. Answer D.

questions.q: SECTION F — There are fourteen .......... in my school.
questions.exp: "Classes" is the correct plural of "class" (a group of pupils). Answer B.

questions.q: He is in the .......... office.
questions.exp: "Principal's office" — the possessive form shows the office belongs to the p...

questions.q: Who is at the door? It's ..........
questions.exp: After "It's," the object pronoun "me" is used (not the subject "I"). Answer C.

questions.q: My brother and his wife love .......... very much.
questions.exp: "Each other" is used for two people. They love each other. Answer B.

questions.q: It pays to be ..........
questions.exp: "Humble" (adjective) is the correct form here — it pays to be humble. Answer A.

questions.q: There is .......... salt in the soup.
questions.exp: "Much" is used with uncountable nouns. Salt is uncountable. Answer C.

questions.q: SECTION G (Antonyms) — The road to our school is narrow.
questions.exp: The antonym of "narrow" is "wide." Answer D.

questions.q: I like to see my bedroom neat.
questions.exp: The antonym of "neat" is "untidy." Answer A.

questions.q: This is the entrance to the compound.
questions.exp: The antonym of "entrance" is "exit." Answer B.

questions.q: SECTION H — Jane is .......... than James in English.
questions.exp: Comparing two people requires the comparative form: "better." Answer B.

questions.q: .......... is the best policy.
questions.exp: "Honesty is the best policy." "Honesty" (noun) is needed as the subject. Answ...

questions.q: Grandma calls from England .......... every day.
questions.exp: "Often" fits "every day" context best as a frequency adverb. Answer D.

questions.q: Alima is the .......... child in her class.
questions.exp: Superlative form with "the" = "most intelligent." Answer B.
